message:
chore: add comprehensive beads for production-ready implementation

Reality check (2026-04-06) found ~40% real / ~60% stubs. Created 20
beads (1 epic, 6 features, 13 tasks/bugs) with full dependency graph
to bring the project to completion.

Critical path (P0, must be done first):
- Implement real Docker container management via Bollard
- Fix hardcoded dry_run: true bug
- Refactor executor to run inside containers
- Add non-interactive env vars (CI=true, DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive)

Core features (P1):
- Result persistence to JSONL + working status command
- Async URL validation for validate --check-urls
- Real parallel worker pool
- Wire _remediate parameter
- Integration tests for Docker lifecycle
- E2E test against real ACFS installers

Polish (P2):
- Remediation parser + Claude wiring
- README accuracy verification

Dependency graph ensures correct implementation order:
  Docker impl → Docker executor → parallel pool → E2E tests → README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+# Database
+*.db
+*.db-journal
+*.db-shm
+*.db-wal
+
+# Lock files
+*.lock
+
+# Temporary
+last-touched
+*.tmp
+
+# Local history backups
+.br_history/
+
+# Sync state (local-only, per-machine)
+.sync.lock
+sync_base.jsonl
+
+# Merge artifacts (temporary files from 3-way merge)
+beads.base.jsonl
+beads.base.meta.json
+beads.left.jsonl
+beads.left.meta.json
+beads.right.jsonl
+beads.right.meta.json
+
+# Daemon runtime files
+daemon.lock
+daemon.log
+daemon.pid
+bd.sock
+sync-state.json
+
+# Worktree redirect file
+redirect
+
+# bv (beads viewer) lock file
+.bv.lock

@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+# Beads Project Configuration
+# issue_prefix: automated_flywheel_setup_checker
+# default_priority: 2
+# default_type: task

@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","title":"epic: bring automated_flywheel_setup_checker to production-ready state","description":"Reality check (2026-04-06) revealed ~40% real / ~60% stubs. Docker isolation (the main value prop) is unimplemented. dry_run is hardcoded. validate --check-urls is a stub. status has no persistence. Remediation parser is empty. Goal: a tool that genuinely runs 41 ACFS installers in Docker, validates success, classifies failures, persists results, and suggests remediation — matching the README promises exactly.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.056429529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.056429529Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1","title":"feature: implement real Docker container isolation via Bollard","description":"The tool's entire value proposition is running installers in isolated Docker containers. Currently container.rs returns placeholder strings and bollard is never imported. Must implement: image pull, container create with resource limits (memory_limit, cpu_quota from config), execute curl|bash inside container, capture stdout/stderr/exit code, cleanup container after test. Use the bollard crate already in Cargo.toml. Each installer gets a fresh ubuntu:22.04 (or config-specified) container. Containers must be ephemeral — destroyed after each test regardless of outcome.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.845827531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.845827531Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.845827531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.1","title":"task: implement ContainerManager with real Bollard Docker API calls","description":"Replace the three stub methods in src/runner/container.rs with real Bollard API calls.

create_container(name):
- Pull image if not present (respect pull_policy from config: always/if-not-present/never)
- Create container with: image from config (default ubuntu:22.04), memory_limit, cpu_quota,
  network access, /tmp mounted as tmpfs for installer scratch space
- Set environment: DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive, HOME=/root, PATH includes /usr/local/bin
- Return the real container ID string from Bollard

exec_in_container(container_id, command):
- Use bollard::exec to create and start an exec instance inside the running container
- Stream stdout and stderr separately into String buffers
- Return (exit_code, stdout, stderr) tuple
- Handle timeout via tokio::time::timeout wrapping the exec stream
- On timeout, kill the exec and return a timeout-specific error

cleanup_container(container_id):
- Stop the container (with 10s grace period)
- Remove the container (force=true to handle edge cases)
- Log any cleanup failures but don't propagate them (best-effort)

Must import bollard::{Docker, container::*, exec::*} and connect to the local Docker socket.
The Docker client should be created once in ContainerManager::new() and reused.

Reference: bollard 0.16 API — use Docker::connect_with_local_defaults().","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:14.528090653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:14.528090653Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","docker"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:14.528090653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.2","title":"task: refactor executor.rs to run installers inside Docker containers instead of locally","description":"Currently executor.rs spawns a local bash process to run curl|bash. Must refactor to:

1. Create a fresh Docker container via ContainerManager
2. Execute the curl|bash command INSIDE the container via exec_in_container
3. Capture stdout/stderr/exit_code from the container exec
4. Classify the result using the existing error classifier
5. Cleanup the container via cleanup_container (always, even on failure)

The execution flow becomes:
  create_container → exec_in_container(curl -fsSL URL | bash -s -- FLAGS) → classify → cleanup

Keep the existing retry logic — retries should create a NEW container each attempt.
Keep the existing timeout logic — but apply it to the container exec, not a local process.

The local execution path should be preserved behind a --local flag for development/debugging,
but Docker should be the default.

Key: the executor must pass the installer URL from the checksums entry, not construct it.
The curl|bash pattern is: curl -fsSL 'URL' | bash -s -- [FLAGS]","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:14.608322112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:14.986972708Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","docker"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:14.608322112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:14.986498982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2","title":"feature: fix execution pipeline — dry_run bug, non-interactive flags, remediate wiring","description":"Three critical bugs in the execution pipeline: (1) dry_run: true hardcoded at main.rs:276 regardless of user CLI flags — bun's installer interpreted --dry-run as a version string causing 404. (2) No non-interactive flags passed to installers — rustup failed because it needs -y. Need to add -y, --yes, DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive to container environment. (3) _remediate parameter is underscore-prefixed and completely ignored in check handler. All three must be fixed for the tool to actually test installers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.921951078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.921951078Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.921951078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.1","title":"bug: remove hardcoded dry_run: true from RunnerConfig in check handler","description":"At src/main.rs:276, RunnerConfig has dry_run: true hardcoded with comment
'Run installers with --dry-run for safety'. This DEFEATS THE PURPOSE of the tool.

The user's --dry-run CLI flag (line 67 of main.rs) controls whether to SKIP execution entirely
(the early return at line 246). The RunnerConfig.dry_run field controls whether to pass --dry-run
to the INSTALLER SCRIPT itself.

These are two completely different concepts:
- CLI --dry-run = don't run anything, just print what would be tested (already works)
- RunnerConfig.dry_run = pass --dry-run flag to installer scripts (SHOULD NOT be hardcoded)

Fix: set dry_run: false in RunnerConfig. The CLI --dry-run flag already handles the
'show what would happen' case via the early return. If we want a 'run installers in
safe mode' option, it should be a separate --safe-mode flag, not hijacking dry_run.

Impact of current bug: bun's installer interprets --dry-run as a version string, constructing
URL https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/releases/download/--dry-run/bun-linux-x64.zip → 404.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:15.870364994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:15.870364994Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","critical"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:15.870364994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.2","title":"task: add non-interactive flags and environment for installer execution","description":"Installers that prompt for input will hang and timeout. Must ensure non-interactive
execution in two ways:

1. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES (set in Docker container):
   - DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive (apt-get won't prompt)
   - RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP_PATH_CHECK=yes
   - NONINTERACTIVE=1 (generic flag some scripts check)
   - CI=true (many scripts detect CI and skip prompts)

2. INSTALLER-SPECIFIC FLAGS:
   The executor should append -y or equivalent to the bash invocation. Most ACFS installer
   scripts accept arguments after 'bash -s --'. The standard pattern is:
     curl -fsSL URL | bash -s -- -y
   
   However, not all installers accept -y. The safest approach:
   - Set the environment variables above (works for most)
   - For the curl|bash pattern, use: bash -s -- (no extra flags by default)
   - Let the CI=true and NONINTERACTIVE=1 env vars handle it
   - For known interactive installers (rustup), the ACFS install.sh already passes -y

   The key insight: ACFS's own installer scripts are designed to run non-interactively
   when invoked by the main install.sh. We just need to set the same env vars that
   install.sh sets.

Evidence: rustup failed with 'Unable to run interactively. Run with -y' because the
container had no TTY and no -y flag. Setting CI=true would have fixed this.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:15.935125963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:17.150966660Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","execution"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:17.150511920Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:15.935125963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.3","title":"bug: wire _remediate parameter through to check handler","description":"In src/main.rs, the check command handler receives _remediate: bool (line 225)
with an underscore prefix, meaning it's completely ignored. The remediation module exists and
has real Claude CLI invocation code, but it's never called.

Fix: remove the underscore prefix, and after a test failure (when remediate=true), call
the remediation module to get suggestions. The flow should be:
  test fails → classify error → if remediate { call ClaudeRemediation::suggest(error_context) }
  → display suggestions to user

This depends on the remediation parse_changes fix (separate bead) for full functionality,
but the wiring itself is a simple parameter rename and conditional call.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:16.310705898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:16.310705898Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.3","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:16.310705898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3","title":"feature: implement result persistence and status command","description":"The status command always prints 'No runs recorded yet'. There is zero persistence — no file I/O, no database. Must implement: write test results to a JSONL file after each run (timestamp, installer name, pass/fail, duration, error classification, stderr excerpt). The status command reads the most recent results file and displays a summary. Results directory should be configurable (default: .afsc-results/ or similar). The reporting/jsonl.rs module has structure but needs to actually write to disk.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.977007050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.977007050Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:24.977007050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.1","title":"task: write test results to JSONL file after each check run","description":"After each check run completes, write results to a JSONL file. The reporting/jsonl.rs
module has structure (TestResult types, JsonlReporter) but never writes to disk.

Implementation:
- Results directory: configurable via config, default ~/.local/share/afsc/results/
- Filename pattern: results_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.jsonl
- Each line is a JSON object: {timestamp, installer_name, status: pass|fail|timeout|skip,
  duration_ms, exit_code, error_classification: {category, severity, retryable, confidence},
  stderr_excerpt (first 500 chars), retry_count, sha256_verified: bool}
- Final line is a summary object: {run_id, total, passed, failed, skipped, duration_total_ms,
  timestamp_start, timestamp_end}
- Write atomically: write to .tmp file, rename to final name on completion
- The reporting/jsonl.rs module should be the owner of this logic

This enables the status command and provides CI/CD artifact output.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:58.650668991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:58.650668991Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["persistence","reporting"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:58.650668991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.2","title":"task: implement status command to read and display persisted results","description":"Replace the stub in cmd_status() that always prints 'No runs recorded yet'.

Implementation:
- Find the most recent results JSONL file in the results directory
- Parse the summary line (last line) for the overview
- Human format: table showing each installer, status (pass/fail with color), duration, error type
- JSON format: the full JSONL content as a JSON array
- --detailed flag: also show stderr excerpts for failed installers
- If no results files exist, print a helpful message: 'No runs found. Run: afsc check'
- Support --run-id flag to view a specific historical run (by timestamp prefix match)

Depends on the persistence write task — can't read what doesn't exist.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:59.448620940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:59.569284891Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","persistence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:59.448620940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:59.568651826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.4","title":"feature: implement real URL validation for validate --check-urls","description":"validate --check-urls currently prints 'URL checking not implemented in sync mode'. Must actually make HTTP HEAD requests to each installer URL and report which are reachable vs broken. Use reqwest (already in Cargo.toml). Should be async with configurable concurrency and timeout. Report: URL, HTTP status code, response time. This is valuable for catching upstream URL changes before they break installs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:25.331998084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:25.331998084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.4","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:25.331998084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.4.1","title":"task: implement async URL checking for validate --check-urls","description":"Replace the stub in validate_checksums() that prints 'URL checking not implemented'.

Implementation:
- Use reqwest (already in Cargo.toml) to make HTTP HEAD requests to each installer URL
- Run checks concurrently with a semaphore (limit 10 concurrent requests)
- Timeout: 15s per URL (configurable)
- For each URL, report: installer_name, url, http_status, response_time_ms, reachable: bool
- Treat 200-299 as reachable, 301-399 as redirect (warn), 400+ as broken (error)
- Handle network errors (DNS failure, connection refused, timeout) as broken
- Human output: table with green checkmarks for reachable, red X for broken
- JSON output: array of {name, url, status, response_time_ms, error}

This function needs to be async. Options:
  a) Make validate_checksums async (breaking change to the sync API)
  b) Create a separate async fn check_urls() called from the CLI handler
  c) Use tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on() inside the sync fn

Recommend option (b): keep validate_checksums sync for basic validation, add a new
async check_urls() function called from cmd_validate when --check-urls is passed.

This is valuable for catching upstream URL changes before they break real installs.
ACFS has 41 URLs pointing to GitHub raw content, bun.sh, sh.rustup.rs, etc. — any
of these could change or go down.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:59.997122113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:59.997122113Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["network","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.4.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:59.997122113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5","title":"feature: implement real parallel execution with worker pool","description":"Parallel execution is a semaphore skeleton. Must implement a real worker pool using tokio tasks: spawn N workers (from --parallel flag), each pulls next installer from a channel/queue, runs it in its own Docker container, reports results back. Must handle: concurrent Docker container creation, proper cleanup on failure/timeout, progress reporting across workers, fail-fast mode (cancel remaining on first failure).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.167135055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.167135055Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.167135055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5.1","title":"task: implement worker pool for parallel installer testing","description":"Replace the semaphore skeleton in runner/parallel.rs with a real worker pool.

Implementation using tokio mpsc channel pattern:
1. Create a channel: tx/rx for InstallerTest items
2. Spawn N worker tasks (N = --parallel flag, default 1)
3. Each worker loops: receive test from channel → create Docker container →
   run installer → classify result → send result back via results channel → cleanup
4. Main task: send all tests to tx, then collect results from results rx
5. Progress reporting: print status line as each test completes (name, pass/fail, duration)

Fail-fast mode (--fail-fast):
- When a test fails, signal all workers to stop accepting new work
- Workers finish their current test but don't pick up new ones
- Use a shared AtomicBool or tokio CancellationToken

Concurrency safety:
- Each worker gets its own Docker container (no shared state)
- Results channel handles ordering
- Progress output needs a mutex or single-writer pattern to avoid interleaved lines

Depends on Docker container implementation — workers need real containers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.766762158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.889407928Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["execution","parallel"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.888869300Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.766762158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6","title":"feature: wire up remediation and fix parse_changes stub","description":"The Claude CLI invocation path is real but: (1) _remediate is ignored in check handler — must wire it through. (2) parse_changes() at remediation/claude.rs returns vec![] — must actually parse Claude's output for actionable suggestions. (3) The remediation flow should: on failure → classify error → send context to Claude CLI → parse response → display suggestions (and optionally apply them if auto_commit is enabled). This is experimental/optional so lower priority, but the wiring must work end-to-end.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.242355474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.242355474Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.242355474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6.1","title":"task: wire remediation into check failure path and fix parse_changes stub","description":"Two things to fix:

1. WIRING: In the check command handler, after a test failure when --remediate is enabled:
   - Create a ClaudeRemediation instance
   - Call suggest() with the error context (installer name, stderr, exit code, classification)
   - Display the suggestions to the user
   - If auto_commit is enabled in config, attempt to apply suggestions

2. PARSE_CHANGES STUB: In remediation/claude.rs, parse_changes() returns vec![].
   Must parse Claude's response for actionable items. Expected format from Claude:
   - File modifications (path + diff/content)
   - Shell commands to run
   - Configuration changes
   
   Use a structured prompt that asks Claude to respond in a parseable format (JSON).
   The prompt template in remediation/prompts.rs is already reasonable — just need
   the parser to extract actions from Claude's response.

   Parsing approach: ask Claude to emit JSON with structure:
   {suggestions: [{type: 'fix'|'workaround'|'investigate', description: str,
     commands: [str], files: [{path, content}]}]}
   
   Then parse_changes() extracts the suggestions array.

This is P2 because the tool is useful without remediation — it's a nice-to-have
that adds value for automated repair workflows.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.966435139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.966435139Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude","remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.966435139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.7","title":"task: add integration tests for Docker container lifecycle","description":"The current 226 tests are mostly struct construction. Need real integration tests
that verify Docker container management works end-to-end.

Tests to add (skip if Docker unavailable — use #[ignore] + env var gate):

1. test_container_create_and_cleanup:
   - Create a container with ubuntu:22.04
   - Verify it exists (docker inspect)
   - Cleanup
   - Verify it's gone

2. test_exec_simple_command:
   - Create container
   - Exec 'echo hello' inside it
   - Verify stdout contains 'hello' and exit code is 0

3. test_exec_failing_command:
   - Create container
   - Exec 'exit 42' inside it
   - Verify exit code is 42

4. test_exec_timeout:
   - Create container
   - Exec 'sleep 300' with 2s timeout
   - Verify timeout error is returned
   - Verify container is still cleaned up

5. test_container_resource_limits:
   - Create container with memory_limit='256M'
   - Verify the limit is set (inspect container)

6. test_real_installer_in_container:
   - Create container
   - Run the zoxide installer (small, fast, reliable)
   - Verify exit code 0
   - Cleanup

Gate all tests with: #[cfg(feature = 'docker-tests')] or check for DOCKER_HOST env var.
CI runs these in the e2e-tests.yml workflow which has Docker access.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:30.153145415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:30.603989402Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docker","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.7","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:30.153145415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.7","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:30.603509404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","title":"task: add end-to-end test that runs 3-5 real ACFS installers and verifies results","description":"Add a bash E2E test script that exercises the full workflow:

1. Build the release binary
2. Run: afsc validate (should pass cleanly, zero warnings)
3. Run: afsc check zoxide atuin --parallel 2 --timeout 120
   - These are small, fast, reliable installers
   - Verify exit code 0
   - Verify human output shows 2 passed
4. Run: afsc check zoxide --format jsonl
   - Verify JSONL output is valid JSON per line
   - Verify 'status' field is 'pass'
5. Run: afsc status
   - Verify it shows the most recent run (not 'No runs recorded yet')
   - Verify it lists the installers tested
6. Run: afsc list --format json
   - Verify all 41 installers are present
   - Verify each has url and sha256

This test proves the entire tool works end-to-end against real ACFS data.
Add to scripts/e2e/tests/ alongside existing E2E tests.

Depends on: Docker implementation, dry_run fix, non-interactive flags, persistence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:31.528025958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:33.318782946Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:31.528025958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:31.926021874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:32.847319353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:32.930595230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:33.318333044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
